question: What level of evidence does the generic NeuTra SSM interface have after implementation and fail-closed real-artifact inventory?
focused_test_command: CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=-1 python -m pytest tests/test_general_ssm_contracts.py tests/test_general_ssm_target_builder.py tests/test_general_ssm_filter_registry.py tests/test_neutra_artifact_loader.py tests/test_fixed_transport_hmc_binding.py -q -p no:cacheprovider

checked: metadata carries non-batch SSM static shape and batch-rank policy
fixed-randomness filter acceptance only when explicit
fixed-transport wrapper does not promote fallback value/score authority
target signature and transport hash in manifest
HMC policy label and hash in manifest
fallback authority rejected for XLA binding
five user-named evidence cells found and classified
selected compact notes and JSON summaries hashed
no external artifact loaded without schema/signature compatibility
not_concluded: generic loader can reuse dense IAF artifacts

hard_vetoes.evidence: Phase 6 classified zero external artifacts as reusable.
Phase 7 ran focused CPU-only tests only; no GPU/CUDA, training, or serious HMC command was launched.
Closeout separates implemented interface surfaces from posterior/scientific/default claims.

viable_implemented_surfaces: Generic SSM target identity contracts and stable signatures
Batch-native posterior target adapter for deterministic TensorFlow value/score functions
Filter-program registry for deterministic/fixed-randomness admissibility metadata
Synthetic frozen affine-diagonal NeuTra artifact loader
Synthetic fixed-transport HMC mechanics manifest binding
migration_blockers: Existing dense IAF NeuTra artifacts do not expose the Phase 4 loader schema.
Existing result notes do not carry the new generic SSMTargetContract target_signature.
Several referenced training-state payloads are absent from the reconciled checkout.
The current loader supports only the reviewed affine-diagonal fixture schema, not dense IAF transport payloads.
next_evidence_needed: A reviewed dense-IAF frozen-transport loader schema or an exporter that converts old artifacts into a small signed BayesFilter frozen-transport manifest.
A target-signature bridge from each historical target identity to a generic SSMTargetContract manifest.
Restoration or re-export of missing training-state payloads with SHA-256 verification.
A separate reviewed plan for any real-artifact mechanics canary, GPU NeuTra training, serious HMC validation, or posterior/reference comparison.
